content:
First one checks that the divisibility 
 abc ∣ (ab+1)(bc+1)(ca+1) 
is equivalent to the three congruences
 a∣bc+1, b∣ca+1, c∣ab+1. 
A little case–work shows that up to permutation the only positive‐integer solutions of

 a∣bc+1, b∣ca+1, c∣ab+1

are the four multisets

 {1,1,1}, {1,1,2}, {1,2,3}, {2,3,7}.

Counting ordered triples gives

• {1,1,1} → 1 ordering  
• {1,1,2} → 3 orderings  
• {1,2,3} → 6 orderings  
• {2,3,7} → 6 orderings  

in all 1+3+6+6=16. 

Answer: \boxed{16}.

Counting ordered triples gives

• {2,3,7} → 6 orderings

Answer: \boxed{16}.
